title: Arthraxon rudis
intro: Arthraxon rudis, also known as Chinese star-grass, is a type of perennial grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Arthraxon rudis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and polysaccharides, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Arthraxon rudis is often prepared as an herbal tea, decoction, or extract, and is used in traditional Chinese medicine to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Arthraxon rudis is classified as a type of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Arthraxon and is further categorized as a species of the Arthraxon genus. The species is also known to be part of the subfamily Chloridoideae. Arthraxon rudis is a perennial grass that is native to various regions of the world, including Africa, Asia, and Australia. Its classification also includes being part of the tribe Cynodonteae.
